g.text: Case-Insensitive Header Normalization: Before vs After (http_parse.c)
const char* get_header(const http_request_t *req, const char *name)
comparison | strcmp() — exact match after normalization
Strategy | Parse Cost | Per-Lookup Cost | Total (k lookups)
Normalize at parse | O(H * N) | O(N) strcmp scan | O(H*N + k*N)
strcasecmp per lookup | O(0) | O(N * name_len) | O(k * N * name_len)
Hash map (case-insens) | O(H * N) + alloc | O(1) amortized | O(H*N + k)
H = header count <= 32 | N = name_len <= 127 | k = num lookups per request (typically 3-8)
WINNER for N<=32 | normalize-once | cache-hot 32-entry scan wins vs. hash overhead
RFC 7230 §3.2 | all four are the SAME header
Cost per lookup: O(n * k) compares
tolower() loop in parse_headers() — O(name_len) once
Loop | for i in 0..name_len: name[i] = tolower(name[i])
Cost | O(n) at parse time, paid ONCE per request
name arg must be lowercase constant
Result: ALL 4 variants HIT — 1 code path
